title: UK and France Sign Deal to Curb Channel Crossings
summary: The UK and France have signed a three-year agreement worth £662 million to combat illegal migration across the English Channel. Under the deal, France will deploy at least 50 riot-trained police officers to beaches, along with drones, two helicopters, and a camera system. For the first time, the UK can withdraw or redirect about £100 million of funding after one year if sufficient progress is not made, though specific targets have not been disclosed. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood stated the deal will stop illegal migrants and put smugglers behind bars. However, critics argue that crossings have increased despite previous funding, with over 41,000 arrivals in 2025. The Conservative Party accused the government of giving money with no conditions, while Reform UK called the system a failure. Migrants in camps in France expressed hope for a better life in the UK, citing democracy and protection. The agreement aims to enhance surveillance and interception efforts, but its effectiveness remains uncertain.

background_read: The English Channel is a busy shipping route between France and the UK. For years, people have tried to cross it illegally in small boats. The UK and France have cooperated on border control, but crossings have increased. This new deal aims to strengthen enforcement with more personnel and technology.
Article_Structure: The main points of the article are the signing of a £662 million deal between the UK and France to stop illegal Channel crossings, including deployment of riot police, drones, and helicopters, with a possible withdrawal of £100 million after one year. The purpose is to deter illegal migration and dismantle smuggling networks. Evidence includes the number of crossings (41,472 in 2025) and criticisms from UK politicians. The article cites Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood and quotes from Conservative and Reform UK critics, but does not provide independent verification. The author's credibility is supported by BBC News reputation. Methodology appears to be reporting on official statements and political reactions, with some on-the-ground quotes from migrants.

perspectives.description: Believes the deal will stop illegal migration and save lives.

Claims the system has already failed and more money won't help.
Hope to reach the UK for a better life with democracy and protection.
